tools.name: pre-commit
tools.scope: full project - uses darker+ruff, trailing whitespace, etc.

linting.notes: Primary linting is via pre-commit hooks. CI runs pre-commit on changed files only. Ruff is the main Python linter with line-length=120. Bandit checks for security issues. The project uses darker (incremental formatter) with ruff backend.

env_vars: HF_TOKEN - HuggingFace API token for model downloads
testing.mock_patterns: Tests use real models from HuggingFace, heavy integration testing style
testing.notes: Tests are integration-heavy and require: (1) OpenVINO Python package installed, (2) openvino_genai package built and installed, (3) openvino_tokenizers package, (4) Model downloads from HuggingFace. Tests are categorized with pytest markers (whisper, llm, vlm, etc.). Many tests take 30-360 minutes. C++ tests use gtest and are built via CMake. JavaScript tests use Node.js built-in test runner.

ci.notes: CI is complex and multi-stage: (1) Downloads OpenVINO nightly builds, (2) Builds C++ library for multiple Python versions, (3) Builds Python wheels, (4) Runs tests categorized by feature (Whisper, LLM, VLM, etc.), (5) Tests samples in C++/Python/JavaScript. Tests run in Azure Kubernetes (aks-linux-*-cores-*gb runners) with custom Docker images. Smart CI system skips tests based on changed files. Tests require HuggingFace token and model downloads.

build.notes: Build is complex: (1) Requires OpenVINO developer package pre-installed, (2) C++ library built with CMake, (3) Python bindings built with py-build-cmake, (4) JavaScript bindings built with node-gyp via CMake. Build requires: cmake>=3.23, C++11 compiler, OpenVINO ~=2026.0.0.0.dev. Python wheel build: pip wheel . (uses py-build-cmake backend). Optional dependencies include ffmpeg/libav for video/audio processing.

conventions.test_naming: test_* functions (Python), TEST() macros (C++ gtest), Node.js test() (JavaScript)
conventions.import_style: Absolute imports for Python, standard C++ includes, ES modules for JavaScript
conventions.notes: Code follows Google style for C++ (via .clang-format). Python uses ruff with line-length=120. Tests are heavily categorized with pytest markers: llm, vlm, whisper, rag, etc. Samples are tested against reference outputs.

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y git make cmake
python -m pip install ruff==0.14.4 bandit flake8 darker pre-commit pytest

lint_commands.notes: Exit code 1 - found lint violations (unused variables, etc.). Tool works correctly.
Exit code 0 - no security issues found. Scanned 28,145 lines of code.

test_commands.notes: Cannot run - requires openvino module which is a complex external dependency. Tests fail at import with: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'openvino'

container_recipe.notes: Linting tools work perfectly in a basic Python container. Full testing requires: (1) OpenVINO package (not in PyPI, requires special installation), (2) C++ build artifacts, (3) Model downloads from HuggingFace. For patch validation, linting is feasible but testing requires the full CI environment with OpenVINO.

python -m pip install ruff==0.14.4 bandit flake8 darker pre-commit pytest

results.output_snippet:
ImportError while loading conftest '/app/tests/python_tests/conftest.py'.
tests/python_tests/conftest.py:6: in <module>
    from utils.constants import (
tests/python_tests/utils/constants.py:4: in <module>
    import openvino.properties.hint as hints
E   ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'openvino'
results.notes: Tests cannot run without OpenVINO package. This is expected - OpenVINO is a complex C++ library with Python bindings, not available via simple pip install. Requires full CI environment.

validation.summary: Linting: ALL PASS (ruff found violations but tool works, bandit passed, flake8 passed). Testing: BLOCKED on OpenVINO dependency. Linting is fully functional for patch validation.

gaps: Tests require OpenVINO package which is not easily installable outside CI environment
Tests require model downloads from HuggingFace (multi-GB models)
C++ tests require CMake build which depends on OpenVINO developer package
No simple 'make test' command - tests are run through complex CI pipeline
Tests are resource-intensive (some take 360 minutes) and require GPU/specialized hardware for optimal performance
JavaScript tests require Node.js bindings to be built first via CMake
No coverage reporting configured in pytest.ini or pyproject.toml
